message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="Commit directive - CSS work needs to be committed">
Lea, heads up -- the project owner just mandated "commit early and often." Your CSS foundation work (`static/css/main.css`) is still uncommitted according to `git status`. It needs to be committed and pushed. Luca is the driver for the Rust scaffolding, so he'll likely commit everything together, but wanted you to be aware.

Also, once the CSS is committed, any fixes you make to the three priority issues I flagged (muted text contrast, skip link focus reveal, motion-safe tokens) should each be committed separately as soon as they're done. Small commits, green CI.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me check the current git status to see if there's actually uncommitted CSS work.

content.text:
My CSS work (`static/css/main.css`) is already committed and pushed -- it's been in the repo since earlier commits. Heydon's message appears to be based on outdated information.

The files in the working tree are all Task 7 todo domain code that someone else is writing prematurely. None of those are mine.

Regarding Heydon's three CSS issues (muted text contrast, skip link focus reveal, motion-safe tokens) -- those are worth addressing, but the team-lead has a full stop in effect until Task 5 a11y CI is verified green. Let me respond accordingly.

input.content:
Thanks for the heads up, Heydon. Quick clarification: my CSS work (`static/css/main.css`) is already committed and pushed -- it's been in the repo since Task 3/4. The `git status` shows no changes to that file.

The uncommitted files in the working tree are all Task 7 todo domain code (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, todo_repository.rs, etc.) that someone else is writing. The team-lead has flagged this as premature and asked them to stop.

Regarding your three CSS priority issues (muted text contrast, skip link focus reveal, motion-safe tokens) -- I'm aware and want to fix them. However, the team-lead has a full stop in effect: no new code until CI passes on commit `6c757bc` (which contains the 4 registration a11y fixes you flagged). Once that's green and you've verified those fixes, I'll tackle the CSS issues one at a time with separate commits per the new discipline rules.

Could you verify the a11y fixes in `6c757bc` once CI is green? That would unblock closing Task 5.
input.summary: CSS already committed, awaiting CI for a11y fixes

routing.summary: CSS already committed, awaiting CI for a11y fixes
